rationale: The cited 2005 JMR paper is a close methodological and substantive predecessor: it models sequential cross-selling in consumer banking with a hierarchical Bayesian multivariate probit and latent maturity, while the 2011 paper explicitly states it extends prior cross-selling work beyond next-product prediction. Given this direct continuity in setting, constructs, and modeling lineage (from sequential adoption modeling to dynamic solicitation/DP policy), this is plausibly foundational rather than an incidental mention.
